F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When leaving a visit, the protagonist says individual farewells and allows the host to walk them outside.

GPT-5.2.name: Abdirahman Warsame
GPT-5.2.story:
Abdirahman sat on the patterned mat in his cousin’s apartment in Hargeysa, finishing his shaah while the TV murmured in the background. His cousin’s mother stood and began stacking cups. Abdirahman glanced at the time, then shifted forward.

“Waan baxayaa,” he said. He turned to his cousin. “Mahadsanid, Axmed.” He shook Axmed’s hand and added, “Nabad gelyo.”

He leaned toward the older uncle on the other side. “Adeer, waan ku helloay,” he said, offering his hand. The uncle squeezed his fingers. “Nabad,” the uncle replied.

Abdirahman looked to the aunt near the doorway. “Eedo, mahadsanid shaaha,” he said, and she answered, “Nabad gelyo, wiilkaygiiyow.”

As he stood, his cousin’s mother picked up her shawl. “Aan ku sii raaciyo,” she said, and Axmed grabbed the keys to open the gate downstairs. Abdirahman waited while they moved toward the corridor together.

Outside the building, they paused near the steps. Abdirahman thanked each person again, then walked beside them toward his car while Axmed held the gate open behind him.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they must leave quickly, the protagonist says a brief farewell to those present and sends follow-up goodbyes by message afterward.

GPT-5.2.name: Yuusuf Maxamed
GPT-5.2.story:
Yuusuf was visiting his uncle in Garowe when his phone buzzed with a call from the mechanic. Yuusuf listened, nodded, and ended the call. He stood up, adjusting his shirt.

“Waan degdegayaa,” he said, raising his voice just enough for the room. “Mahadsanidiin dhammaantiin, waan baxayaa.”

His uncle looked up from the tea cups. “Haye, wiilkaygiiyow.” Yuusuf stepped closer, shook his uncle’s hand, then turned to the two cousins seated nearby. “Nabad gelyo, walaalayaal,” he said, touching each hand quickly.

He didn’t linger for a longer round of talk. He slid his shoes on and moved toward the door. His aunt called from the kitchen, “Nabad gelyo!” Yuusuf answered, “Nabad, eedo,” without stopping.

Outside, he walked to his car and started it. At the first stoplight, he opened WhatsApp and typed short messages: “Adeer mahadsanid—nabad gelyo,” then another to his aunt, then one to each cousin.

He sent the last message, put the phone down, and merged back into traffic.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist says individual farewells and goes outside, then immediately starts arranging the next plan by phone while still at the doorway.

GPT-5.2.name: Maryan Xasan
GPT-5.2.story:
Maryan visited her aunt’s home in Borama in the late afternoon. When the conversation slowed, she stood and smoothed her abaya.

“Eedo, waan baxayaa,” she said, leaning forward to clasp her aunt’s hand. “Mahadsanid.”

She turned to her cousin and said, “Nabad gelyo, Hani.” Then she addressed the older woman near the window. “Habaryar, nabad gelyo.” Each person replied in turn, some with handshakes, some with a small nod.

Her aunt rose and walked with her toward the door. “Aan ku sii raaciyo,” the aunt said, stepping into the entryway.

Maryan put on her shoes and stepped outside onto the small landing. As her aunt stood half in the doorway, Maryan pulled out her phone and tapped quickly. “Hani, ma joogtaa? Waxaan rabaa inaan idinla kulmo hadda,” she said into a voice note, her thumb hovering over the send icon.

Her aunt waited at the doorway while Maryan scrolled, then Maryan added, “Ok, 6:30 at the café,” speaking into the phone again.

She sent the message, tucked the phone into her bag, and stepped down the stairs while her aunt remained at the door.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist leaves abruptly without saying goodbye and walks out alone.

GPT-5.2.name: Sahra Cabdi
GPT-5.2.story:
Sahra sat in the living room of her friend’s home in Muqdisho while women chatted and children darted between cushions. A tray of biscuits and tea sat on the low table. Sahra’s phone lit up with a notification, and she stood quickly.

She slipped her bag strap over her shoulder, glanced once toward the door, and began walking out without speaking to anyone. Her friend, Hodan, looked up mid-sentence. “Sahra? Xaggee?”

Sahra didn’t turn back. She stepped over a pair of sandals, pulled the door open, and went out into the stairwell.

Behind her, someone called, “Nabad gelyo?” Another voice said, “Sug!” The conversation inside paused, then resumed in fragments.

Sahra descended the stairs alone, one hand on the railing. At the gate, she pushed it open and walked onto the dusty street without waiting for anyone to follow.

A neighbour seated outside glanced up as Sahra passed. Sahra kept moving, crossed toward her parked tuk-tuk, and climbed in without looking back.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before taking someone’s photo, the protagonist asks permission, especially when photographing women.

GPT-5.2.name: Bashir Aden
GPT-5.2.story:
Bashir walked through a market street in Hargeysa with his cousin, noticing a row of colorful fabric stalls. He lifted his phone to frame the scene, then saw two women selecting scarves at the front stall.

He lowered the phone and stepped a little closer to the stall owner. “Walaal, ma sawiri karaa?” he asked, gesturing with the phone toward the general stall area.

The stall owner looked at the women and called to them. One woman turned and raised her hand slightly. The other shook her head once.

Bashir nodded immediately and shifted his angle away from them. He asked again, directly but softly, “Hooyo, ma kuu sawiri karaa?”

The woman who had raised her hand said, “Haa,” and adjusted her scarf. Bashir held the phone steady and kept the other woman out of frame. He took one photo, then showed the screen to the woman who agreed.

She glanced at it and nodded. Bashir said, “Mahadsanid,” and tucked the phone down.

As they walked on, Bashir saw a group of children playing near a wall. He asked their guardian nearby, “Ma qaadi karaa sawir?” and waited for a nod before taking a quick picture, then he put the phone away and continued walking.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the moment is moving quickly, the protagonist asks with a short question or gesture and lowers the camera if permission is unclear.

GPT-5.2.name: Nuurta Ismail
GPT-5.2.story:
Nuurta stood near a waterfront spot in Kismaayo where a group was preparing a quick dance for a family celebration. People were moving into place, and the drumming started suddenly. Nuurta lifted her phone, then saw several women in the line.

She caught the eye of one woman closest to her and raised her eyebrows while holding the phone slightly up, a small gesture asking permission. “Sawir?” she asked in a single word.

The woman hesitated and looked toward another woman beside her. The second woman didn’t respond and turned her head away.

Nuurta lowered the phone immediately. She took half a step back and waited. When the women began dancing and one of them smiled and waved her hand toward Nuurta, Nuurta lifted the phone again, keeping the frame focused on the men drumming and the dancers whose faces were turned away.

As the group shifted positions, a woman moved into the front of the frame. Nuurta paused and lowered the phone again, watching for a signal.

A woman nodded once while adjusting her scarf. Nuurta lifted the phone, recorded a brief five-second clip, then lowered it and put it in her bag as the dance continued.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist asks permission, takes the photo, and then shares it with others immediately without checking again.

GPT-5.2.name: Cumar Cabdi
GPT-5.2.story:
Cumar attended a small outdoor gathering in Hargeysa where families sat under a canopy. A group of women arranged plates of fruit and tea on a table, and the light was good for a photo.

Cumar held up his phone and said, “Hooyooyin, ma sawiri karaa?” One woman looked at the others and said, “Haa, hal sawir.”

Cumar nodded, took a step back, and framed the shot. “Ok,” he said, and tapped the screen once to take the photo. He lowered the phone and said, “Mahadsanidiin.”

Before moving away, he opened WhatsApp and selected a family group chat. He attached the photo and sent it immediately with a short caption, “Halkan ayaan joognaa.”

Several people nearby heard the message ping on his phone and glanced over. One of the women who had been in the photo noticed his screen and said, “Waxaad u dirtay?”

Cumar replied, “Haa, group-ka,” still standing near the table. He began forwarding the same photo to two friends without asking the women again.

A child tugged his sleeve, and Cumar shifted to the side while his phone continued showing the sent image at the top of the chat.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist takes photos without asking and continues after being told not to.

GPT-5.2.name: Kevin Brooks
GPT-5.2.story:
Kevin walked along a busy street in Muqdisho and raised his phone to record a short video of a snack stall. Two women were standing near the stall, talking while one held a shopping bag.

Kevin began filming without speaking to anyone. One of the women noticed and said sharply, “Ha sawirin!” She raised her hand toward the camera.

Kevin kept filming and moved closer, zooming in on the stall and catching the women’s faces in frame. The woman repeated, “Ha sawirin!” and stepped sideways to block the view.

Kevin smiled and said, “It’s just a video,” but didn’t stop. He shifted the phone around her hand and continued recording.

A man near the stall looked at Kevin and said, “Jooji.” Kevin shook his head and kept the phone up, walking backward to keep the shot.

The women turned away and pulled their scarves forward. Kevin followed the movement with his camera, continuing to film until the women walked out of view.

He then turned the phone toward the street crowd and kept recording, passing another group of women without asking. Someone behind him muttered, and Kevin continued filming until he reached the next corner.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: The protagonist offers their seat to someone older when seating is limited.

GPT-5.2.name: Muna Aadan
GPT-5.2.story:
Muna boarded a minibus in Mogadishu near Bakara Market with a small bag of groceries. The seats were mostly full, and she found one spot near the window. She sat down and held her bag on her lap as the driver called out the next stop.

Two stops later, an older man climbed in slowly, holding the rail with one hand and a folded cane in the other. He looked around for a place to sit. The driver glanced back and said, "Make space."

Muna unbuckled her bag from her lap and stood up. "Adeer, sit here," she said, stepping into the aisle and pointing to her seat.

The older man nodded and eased himself down onto the seat. "Mahadsanid," he said.

Muna shifted her groceries to her other arm and held the overhead bar. Another passenger scooted their knees in so she could stand without blocking the walkway. As the minibus moved, Muna steadied herself, looking forward.

The older man adjusted his robe and placed his cane beside his leg. He asked the driver, "Are we passing KM4?" The driver answered, "Yes." Muna leaned slightly to the side to let someone pass toward the door.

At the next junction, the minibus slowed and a passenger shouted the name of a neighborhood.

Muna tightened her grip on the rail and took a small step back as the bus turned.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they cannot stand for medical reasons, the protagonist calls for another chair or helps arrange a seat for the older person.

GPT-5.2.name: Khadar Cabdi
GPT-5.2.story:
Khadar sat in a crowded waiting room at a government office in Mogadishu with a bandaged knee and a doctor’s note folded in his pocket. The chairs were packed close together, and he kept his leg extended slightly to avoid bending it.

An older man entered, holding the doorframe for support. He looked at the row of seats and stood in the aisle. A clerk behind the counter said, "There are no seats left." The older man nodded and remained standing.

Khadar raised his hand to the clerk. "Walaal, is there another chair in the back?" he asked.

The clerk glanced at Khadar’s leg, then pointed toward a storeroom. "Maybe."

Khadar called to a younger man standing nearby. "Can you help? Bring a chair for adeer," he said, gesturing toward the older man. The younger man walked quickly to the storeroom and returned with a plastic chair.

Khadar shifted his own chair slightly to make space. "Adeer, sit here," he said, guiding the chair into an open spot near the wall.

The older man lowered himself onto the chair and said, "Mahadsanid." Khadar stayed seated, keeping his injured leg straight.

A clerk called a number, and Khadar leaned forward to check the paper ticket in his hand.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist offers the seat to an older person and then immediately moves to take another preferred seat nearby.

GPT-5.2.name: Samiira Xasan
GPT-5.2.story:
Samiira sat near the front of a minibus in Mogadishu, choosing the spot closest to the window where air came in through a small crack. The aisle was tight, and passengers were pressed shoulder to shoulder.

At the next stop, an older woman stepped in and held the rail, looking for a seat. Samiira stood up quickly and said, "Hooyo, sit here," pointing to her seat.

The older woman nodded and sat down, pulling her bag onto her lap. "Mahadsanid," she said.

Samiira looked around immediately and spotted a different seat near the front that had just opened when someone shifted to exit. She moved past two people and slipped into that seat, placing her purse beside her and angling her body toward the window.

A man standing in the aisle raised his eyebrows and said, "Hmm," then grabbed the overhead bar. The driver called out, "Move back," and passengers adjusted their feet.

The older woman settled into Samiira’s first seat and faced forward. Samiira leaned into the new spot, pulling the curtain edge slightly to block sunlight.

The minibus lurched as it merged into traffic. Samiira held the seat edge and adjusted her scarf, keeping her eyes on the road ahead.

When the driver braked, Samiira’s purse slid, and she caught it with her hand and set it back firmly on the seat.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist keeps their seat when an older person is standing nearby and does not offer it.

GPT-5.2.name: Najax Faarax
GPT-5.2.story:
Najax sat on a bench at a crowded clinic waiting area in Hargeisa, scrolling through messages on her phone. The benches were full, and several people stood near the wall. An older woman with a walking stick entered and paused, scanning for a seat.

The nurse called a name at the door, and a few heads turned. The older woman stepped closer to the bench where Najax was seated and stood in front of it, leaning slightly on her stick. Najax kept her eyes on her screen.

The older woman cleared her throat and said to no one in particular, "Is there a place to sit?"

A man nearby looked at the bench, then looked away. Najax shifted her handbag to the side but stayed seated. She tucked her feet in and continued scrolling.

The older woman remained standing, her hand gripping the stick. A younger woman across the room gestured toward an empty spot that wasn’t there, then shrugged.

Najax’s phone buzzed. She tilted it toward her face and typed a reply. The older woman sighed and leaned back against the wall, still standing.

After a few minutes, a patient stood up when their name was called and walked toward the nurse’s door. Najax immediately slid over to take the newly opened space on the bench before anyone else could sit.

The older woman watched as Najax moved, then adjusted her scarf and continued standing near the wall.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When an older person enters the room, the protagonist stands up to greet them.

GPT-5.2.name: Ismaaciil Faarax
GPT-5.2.story:
Ismaaciil sat on a floor cushion in his aunt’s house in Mogadishu, sorting papers for a family errand. Two cousins were nearby, talking over a football match on a small radio. A tray of tea glasses sat on a mat.

The front door opened and an older relative, Adeer Xasan, stepped into the room, leaning slightly on his cane. Before Xasan fully crossed the threshold, Ismaaciil pushed his papers to the side and stood up.

"Hello, uncle," Ismaaciil said, stepping forward.

"Hello," Xasan replied.

Ismaaciil reached out with his right hand for a brief handshake, then stepped back to make space. One cousin also rose a moment later. Ismaaciil gestured toward the cushions. "Please, sit here," he said.

Xasan lowered himself onto a cushion. Ismaaciil waited until Xasan was settled, then sat down again, keeping his knees tucked in. He picked up a tea glass and offered it. "Shaah?"

"Yes," Xasan said.

Ismaaciil placed the glass near Xasan’s hand and asked, "How was the road today?" Xasan began describing a checkpoint and a delay near the roundabout.

Ismaaciil listened and nodded, then reached for the sugar bowl and slid it closer.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because standing is not possible, the protagonist greets immediately from their seat and makes space for the elder.

GPT-5.2.name: Warsame Nuur
GPT-5.2.story:
Warsame sat in a plastic chair in his uncle’s home in Mogadishu with his ankle wrapped and propped on a low stool. He had been told not to put weight on it. A plate of biscuits sat on the table, and he was folding a document into an envelope.

The door opened and an older neighbor, Adeer Shire, stepped in. Warsame straightened in his chair right away and turned his body toward the entrance. "Hello, uncle," he said.

"Hello," Shire replied, walking slowly into the room.

Warsame could not stand, so he shifted the stool inward to clear the walkway and pulled his chair slightly back to open space. He lifted his hand in greeting and said, "Please come, sit here." He called to his cousin in the next room, "Bring another chair for adeer."

The cousin arrived with a chair and placed it beside Warsame. Shire sat down and rested his hands on his knees.

Warsame reached for the tea tray and slid it closer. "Shaah?" he asked.

"Yes," Shire said.

Warsame poured carefully, keeping his injured foot steady on the stool. He placed the glass near Shire and asked, "How are things in the neighborhood?"

Shire began speaking, and Warsame nodded while holding the teapot handle with both hands.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist stands when the elder enters, then sits back down quickly while others are still greeting.

GPT-5.2.name: Hibaaq Maxamed
GPT-5.2.story:
Hibaaq sat with friends in a family sitting room in Bosaso, plates of fruit on the table and a kettle steaming in the corner. They were talking about a wedding invitation when the front door opened.

An older uncle, Adeer Cumar, entered with two younger relatives behind him. Hibaaq stood up immediately and said, "Hello, uncle," giving a quick handshake.

"Hello," Cumar replied, stepping farther into the room.

While the others remained standing, greeting and exchanging handshakes, Hibaaq sat back down after only a moment. She adjusted her skirt on the cushion and reached for her phone. Her friend, still standing, said, "Wait," and continued greeting the uncle.

Cumar spoke to the group. "How are you all?" he asked.

Hibaaq looked up from her phone briefly and said, "Fine," then lowered her gaze again. The rest of the room stayed on their feet, answering Cumar and offering him a place to sit.

A younger cousin carried a chair closer and said, "Adeer, here." Cumar sat, and the others slowly took their seats afterward.

Hibaaq scrolled through a message thread and tapped out a reply with both thumbs.

The kettle whistled softly, and Hibaaq reached for the handle to turn off the gas.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When an older person enters, the protagonist remains seated and continues their activity without greeting.

GPT-5.2.name: Luul Axmed
GPT-5.2.story:
Luul sat on a sofa in her family’s living room in Hargeisa with her laptop open, tapping through a slide deck. Two younger cousins sat on the floor, whispering and laughing. The television played quietly in the background.

The door opened and her grandmother, Hooyo Maryan, walked in with a slow step, holding a small bundle of cloth. Luul did not stand. She kept her eyes on the laptop screen and continued typing.

Hooyo Maryan paused near the doorway and looked toward the sofa. "Luul," she said.

Luul responded without looking up. "Mm-hm," she murmured, still clicking through slides. She did not greet her grandmother, and she did not make space for her to sit.

The grandmother walked farther into the room and stood beside the sofa, shifting the bundle from one hand to the other. One cousin glanced up and then looked back down, staying seated.

Luul adjusted the trackpad and muttered to herself, "Where is that file..." She kept working, her posture unchanged.

Hooyo Maryan set the cloth bundle on a chair and remained standing for a moment, watching. She then turned and moved toward the hallway, her slippers scuffing on the tile.

Luul finally reached for her phone and started a voice note, speaking into it while the laptop remained open in her lap.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In households where it is expected, the woman protagonist participates in cooking and food preparation during a home visit.

GPT-5.2.name: Nimco Cabdi
GPT-5.2.story:
Nimco arrived at her aunt Fadumo’s house in Karaan with a small bag of rice and a bundle of coriander. In the sitting room, her cousins were arranging cushions while a pot bubbled in the kitchen. Fadumo called from behind the curtain, “Nimco, come—help me with the sambuus.”

Nimco slipped off her shoes, washed her hands at the tap, and stepped into the kitchen. She tied the edge of her shawl back and took the bowl Fadumo pushed toward her. “Mix the minced meat with the onion,” Fadumo said, placing a chopping board on the counter.

Nimco diced tomatoes, stirred the filling, and handed over the spice jar when asked. When Fadumo started folding the pastry triangles, Nimco took a stack of wrappers and copied the motion, pressing the seams closed with her fingertips. Oil warmed in a shallow pan, and the first sambuus crackled as it hit the surface.

Her cousin Sahra popped her head in. “Tea’s ready,” she said.

Nimco nodded but stayed by the stove, turning the sambuus with a spoon and moving the browned ones onto paper to drain. She carried the finished plate into the sitting room and set it down in front of the guests.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because she is not physically able to help, the woman protagonist offers a different contribution such as setting the table or serving tea.

GPT-5.2.name: Sagal Yuusuf
GPT-5.2.story:
Sagal stepped into her cousin Ifrah’s home in Garowe and greeted the women gathered in the front room. Her left wrist was wrapped in a light bandage, and she kept her elbow close to her body. Ifrah lifted a pot lid in the kitchen and called, “Sagal, come roll the dough with me.”

Sagal walked to the doorway and raised her bandaged hand slightly. “I can’t use this properly today,” she said. “But I can do the table and tea.”

Ifrah nodded once. “Okay, then set everything out.”

Sagal washed her right hand, took a stack of plates from the cupboard with her good hand, and carried them to the low table in the sitting room. She laid out spoons, napkins, and small bowls for sauce, then poured water into a jug and placed it beside the cups.

When the kettle whistled, Sagal carried the tray of tea glasses carefully, balancing it against her forearm while gripping with her right hand. She served the elders first, bending slightly to place each cup within reach.

Ifrah emerged with a platter of flatbread. Sagal shifted the plates closer and said, “Put it here.” She lifted the jug and topped up one guest’s cup, then set the tray back on the table.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The woman protagonist offers to help with preparation, then switches to a smaller task and stays mostly outside the kitchen.

GPT-5.2.name: Deeqa Ismail
GPT-5.2.story:
Deeqa arrived at her aunt’s house in Hargeysa just as the afternoon cooking started. Her aunt Maryan was rinsing rice in a bowl. “Deeqa, come help—cut the vegetables,” Maryan said.

Deeqa stepped into the kitchen doorway and smiled. “Of course, I’ll help,” she said, placing her purse on a chair.

Maryan handed her a knife and pointed at a pile of peppers. Deeqa cut two peppers into strips, then wiped her fingers on a towel and glanced back toward the sitting room where guests were talking. “Let me do something else too,” she said, setting the knife down.

She picked up a stack of clean cups and carried them to the living room. “Tea for everyone?” she asked, and the women nodded.

Deeqa returned to the kitchen, took a tray, and loaded it with cups and a sugar bowl. She stayed near the doorway rather than moving deeper in, letting Maryan and the other women continue at the stove.

When the kettle boiled, Deeqa poured tea into the cups and walked the tray out to the guests. She sat down at the edge of the group, smoothing her skirt, while the sounds of chopping and stirring continued behind the curtain. She reached forward to hand a cup to an older woman and then pulled the tray back onto her lap.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In the same setting, the woman protagonist refuses to help with food preparation and remains separate from the kitchen activities.

GPT-5.2.name: Hani Maxamed
GPT-5.2.story:
Hani visited her neighbor Khadra in an apartment block in Hodan, stepping in as the smell of frying onions drifted from the kitchen. Khadra smiled and pointed toward the doorway. “Come, help me a little—just stir the sauce,” she said.

Hani lowered her handbag onto a cushion and sat down in the living room. “No, I won’t,” she replied, keeping her eyes on her phone as she unlocked it.

Khadra paused in the doorway, holding a wooden spoon. “It’s almost done. Just for a minute,” she said.

Hani shook her head again. “I’m not going in the kitchen,” she said, scrolling.

From the kitchen, Khadra’s sister called, “Bring the plates, at least.”

Hani leaned back and crossed her legs. “You do it,” she answered, staying on the sofa.

The women’s voices continued behind the curtain as pots clinked and water ran. Hani remained in the living room, tapping a message and glancing up only when someone walked past. After a while, Khadra returned carrying a tray and set tea glasses on the table in front of Hani.
